title: FOBSS V-detector Second-Dataset Validation — Pre-Registration

dataset.name: FOBSS (Frequent Observations from a Battery System with Subunits)
dataset.reference: Steinbuss et al. (2019), e-Energy ACM conference
dataset.description: Monitoring data from a stationary modular battery test bench. 44 cells total in 4 series-connected slave boards of 11 cells each. Multiple experiment protocols (jump profiles, constant-current profiles, internal resistance measurements). Real test-bench data, NOT field EV data.

context_vs_battgp.battgp: Field EV warranty-return data, 27 systems, 8 cells/system, GP-inferred degradation reference
context_vs_battgp.fobss: Lab test-bench, 1 physical pack / 53 experiment runs, 44 cells, NO known faults or degradation labels
context_vs_battgp.scientific_purpose: Test whether V-detector agrees with trivial baseline on a HEALTHY series pack — different chemistry, different application, no confounding from mixed degradation states
context_vs_battgp.limitation_acknowledged: Cannot assess fault detection accuracy (no ground-truth fault labels). Tests false-positive rate and cross-experiment consistency only.

experiment_inclusion_criteria.rule: Include experiment directories where |I_mean_on_discharge_segment| >= 5.0 A AND n_high_current_rows >= 200
experiment_inclusion_criteria.note: 5A threshold captures both 10A and 25A discharge protocols while excluding rest/low-current segments

vdetector_protocol.algorithm: NSA variable-radius LOO (same as BattGP experiment)
vdetector_protocol.v_normalization: min-max per experiment per cell (same as BattGP)

power_gate.description: Median LOO gap / p95 pairwise delta-gap >= 2.0 to confirm a detectable weak cell
power_gate.note: Threshold lowered from BattGP 10x to 2x because healthy packs have smaller natural imbalance signals

vdetector_protocol.trivial_baseline: argmin(mean V_norm per cell over high-current rows)

primary_metric: Fraction of passing experiments where V-detector and trivial baseline identify the SAME weakest cell

H1_agreement.description: V-detector agrees with trivial baseline on weakest cell identification
H1_agreement.rationale: Healthy pack with no degradation heterogeneity -> trivial baseline and V-detector both capture same natural imbalance signal

H2_consistency.description: V-detector identifies the SAME cell as weakest across >= 80% of passing experiments
H2_consistency.rationale: One physical pack -> one physically weakest cell from manufacturing variation

H3_power_gate_pass.description: Power gate (gap ratio >= 2.0) passes in at least 40% of experiments
H3_power_gate_pass.rationale: Natural cell imbalance in a healthy pack may be too small for gap ratio >= 10x (BattGP threshold) but should exceed 2x in most discharge experiments

interpretation_rules.if_H1_passes: High agreement on healthy data confirms V-detector tracks same signal as trivial baseline when no degradation confounds exist. The BattGP 75% agreement (below this) is then interpretable as degradation-driven divergence, not algorithm noise.
interpretation_rules.if_H1_fails: V-detector produces spurious weak-cell identifications even on healthy data. This would undermine the BattGP results by suggesting algorithm instability is a confounder.
interpretation_rules.if_H2_passes: Consistent cell ID across experiments confirms the detection is reproducible, not run-dependent randomness.
interpretation_rules.note: This experiment cannot validate fault DETECTION capability. It validates consistency and false-alarm rate on known-healthy data.
